item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Confirming that Anorrack has permission from the author to edit the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one, mainly because it had dinosaurs. However, I don't like the part at the end about how variants without a mate were killed off by predators. Realistically, I don't think the Foundation would let unique variants be destroyed. Rather, I'd imagine they'd keep them in storage lockers or in their own containment cells in order to study them and prevent them from being harmed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Late to the party but I like the SCP, but is this supposed to be this way? "Researcher H.: One last question. Was this

V-09: yes. how did you know?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Acrylic paleoart is created by Yusuke Ikeda https://www.facebook.com/yusuke.ikeda.1485

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Redacting the amount of time for “breeding” seems illogical. Wouldn’t it be best to know how long it takes so as to prevent the creation of unintended instances?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really cute article. I can see how it could become spook if they kept asking it to give them horrible things, but at the moment it's just good adorable fun.
10/10 would visit tiny plastic jurassic park.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ARC

Is this a Primeval reference?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that this page's tags were removed, as there were a few tags missing.

Author, if you're not going to add *all* the relevant tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have *no* tags. Adding only a few tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

item.title: Re: Junior Crit Staff-Post - Information
item.content:encoded: I get what youre saying, and sorry if im coming off as not being open to ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you man, thats a great idea! I will definitely tweak it but i love the idea of it making people feel "playful".

item.title: Re: Junior Crit Staff-Post - Information
item.content:encoded:
Well, if this gets deleted, be sure to have copied the source of your article into your sandbox. Keep in mind, friend, that the mainlist is a place for summary judgement of finished works.

Honestly, the whole process should be done within the Wiki and Sandbox because this is your audience. We have the I&B board so that an idea can be refined before drafting, and during/after drafting, we have the D&C to help refine the article.

Throughout those steps, there is also the immensely helpful Forum Crit Team who are, mostly, available on the IRC chatroom (location: #thecritters) or who accept private messages.

So, with all that said, your idea is actually quite an interesting one, but it can be taken further. However, if it gets deleted, send me a Private Message, and I'd be willing to give some pointers on how to tone it up conceptually and, hopefully, give you the overall direction you're looking for.

Personal note here — never hold so tightly to an idea that you close off the idea of its transformation. You may be closing it off to the best and brightest writers on the `net. Almost all works here on the wiki are, in one form or another, products of collaboration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got an idea…

"The SCP seems to have a strange aura surrounding it, measuring approximately 5 meters. Anyone who enters said aura claims to feel an extreme pressure to 'ask for a toy' from SCP-4118. This aura is created by unkown methods, possibly a new form of radiation, and shall be investigated further."

"Any and all attempts to destroy instances of SCP-4118-1 result in failure, and are met with hostility towards staff. However, all SCP-4118-1s attempts of harming staff result in minor punctures and scratches due to their small nature."

If you can make it seem strange like that, without explaining it in too much detail, then this could be a great SCP. You definitely have my permission to take those quotes, btw. Just make sure that all details you add explain only what's needed, any more and it seems like a comic instead of an SCP. Make us feel like the scientists, make us want to know more, you definitely are capable of it.

Adding detail to a story is a great thing, don't get me wrong, but adding too much makes it easier to get the idea, thus turning people away. If people don't completely understand what it is they are looking at, but know the idea, they will read more to find out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thats the problem im running into. The original thing had that, where you didn't know how or why it did this, but it did and something was special enough for people to try and steal it. But everyone wanted me to flesh it out more and more and now you know everything about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the idea, but there isn't really a hook. A good safe SCP done right is like SCP 999. With SCPs like 999, you get this sensation of curiosity, you want to learn more, it is strange, and you want to figure it all out. With this, you just know what it is. There isn't enough mystery, but it has potential to be good. Try to lure people in with mystery and keep them in with the awe of the pure strangeness.

item.title: Re: Junior Crit Staff-Post - Information
item.content:encoded: I did follow them and i did do these steps, just not here on the wiki. And I keep hearing the refining bit but ive generally gotten no direction and theres nowhere to really "flesh out" without tarnishing the original idea. I get the tone issues and i will try to fix but thats about all i can do.

item.title: Junior Crit Staff-Post - Information
item.content:encoded:
No I&B thread. No Draft thread. Make sure you read and follow the articles linked in step one (listed below):

Typically, cold-posted articles don't survive very long. For the best chances of having an article that survives the mainlist, follow these steps:

  1. Give the following pages a read: Guide for Newbies and How To Write an SCP.
  2. Copy the source of your cold-posted article into your own Sandbox page (instructions for setting up your sandbox page on frontpage of sandbox).
  3. Write up a brief summary of your concept in the Ideas and Brainstorming forum and get critiques on your concept.
  4. Once the concept is sufficiently critiqued, start drafting! When you get to a good enough point to where it can be critiqued, toss a link to your sandbox page in the Drafts and Critiques forum for crits on your draft.

That said, this isn't a bad idea. It just needs quite a bit of refining, as to the points mentioned above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same as the others, there are tone issues, the cross reference is distracting, and overall it could be more fleshed out.

Specifically though, in your description, you describe it a some tube, like one toys are normally packaged in. This idea is not consistent throughout the world and time, and many folks will see this and have no idea what you are talking about. Try to describe things without using comparisons, and or something like this, you may want to try to get a picture to help out.

Downvote, but the idea isn't bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with the general sentiment that it's not a bad idea, but needs to be more fleshed out. +1.

Also, you should do some more proofreading — there are a bunch of punctuation errors scattered through the document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the encouragement! I definitely want to work on it a bit more.

and you may be correct!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this concept, but I think it needs to be more fleshed out. That, and there are a few gaps in your clinical tone. Overall though I liked this one even if I also think it needs improvement

So +1 for now, if only because I don't think the core idea deserves to be deleted.

PS: 'ARC'? Is that a new GoI I smell?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the tip. I'm going to try to give this a little more depth, although i might abandon the 1000 reference since it might be too inserted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has the potential to be good, but this doesn't do nearly enough, and the SCP-1000 reference isn't well-developed enough to work.

-1
